title: Grangea sphaeranthus
intro: Grangea maderaspatana, commonly known as Grangea sphaeranthus, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Grangea sphaeranthus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.

It belongs to the tribe Vernonieae, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Grangea sphaeranthus is native to Africa, and its classification places it within the family Asteraceae. This plant is often classified under the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics. Grangea sphaeranthus is also classified under the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants and their allies.
